findings.evidence: grep hit: `342:    change (S.operator t₀) ((S.operator (t - t₀)) x) = (S.operator t) x`.
findings.fix: Replace the `change` with an explicit lemma/equality for applying the semigroup law, e.g. prove `S.operator t x = S.operator t₀ (S.operator (t - t₀) x)` from `h_sg` and rewrite/congr with that.
findings.issue: `strongContAt` uses `change` to reshape the goal after `h_sg`, relying on CLM coercion/comp definitional equality; the comment says it is a defeq reshape, but not why this is unavoidable.

findings.evidence: grep hit: `663:    change Continuous (Set.piecewise (Set.Ici 0) f (fun _ => x))`.
findings.fix: Introduce an explicit equality `hg_piecewise : g = Set.piecewise (Set.Ici 0) f (fun _ => x)` by extensionality/simp, then rewrite with it before `continuous_piecewise`, or define `g` as `Set.piecewise` from the start.
findings.issue: `tendsto_average_resolvent_integrand` changes `Continuous g` into continuity of `Set.piecewise ...`, depending on the current definitional equality between the local `if` definition of `g` and `Set.piecewise`.

summary: The main proof issue is reliance on undocumented definitional reshaping in two nontrivial proofs. Replace the `change` steps with explicit equalities/rewrites so these proofs do not depend on current wrapper/coercion transparency.
